d = {}
t1.__eq = function () return true end
t1.__lt = function () return true end
t1.__le = function () return false end
setmetatable(d, t1)
assert(c == d and c < d and not(d <= c))
t2 = {}
t2.__eq = t1.__eq
t2.__lt = t1.__lt
setmetatable(d, t2)
assert(c == d and c < d and not(d <= c))

file.content:
-- $Id: testes/events.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

print('testing metatables')

local debug = require'debug'

X = 20; B = 30

_ENV = setmetatable({}, {__index=_G})

collectgarbage()

X = X+10
assert(X == 30 and _G.X == 20)
B = false
assert(B == false)
_ENV["B"] = undef
assert(B == 30)

assert(getmetatable{} == nil)
assert(getmetatable(4) == nil)
assert(getmetatable(nil) == nil)
a={name = "NAME"}; setmetatable(a, {__metatable = "xuxu",
                    __tostring=function(x) return x.name end})
assert(getmetatable(a) == "xuxu")
assert(tostring(a) == "NAME")
-- cannot change a protected metatable
assert(pcall(setmetatable, a, {}) == false)
a.name = "gororoba"
assert(tostring(a) == "gororoba")

local a, t = {10,20,30; x="10", y="20"}, {}
assert(setmetatable(a,t) == a)
assert(getmetatable(a) == t)
assert(setmetatable(a,nil) == a)
assert(getmetatable(a) == nil)
assert(setmetatable(a,t) == a)


function f (t, i, e)
  assert(not e)
  local p = rawget(t, "parent")
  return (p and p[i]+3), "dummy return"
end

t.__index = f

a.parent = {z=25, x=12, [4] = 24}
assert(a[1] == 10 and a.z == 28 and a[4] == 27 and a.x == "10")

    return debug.getuservalue(a) == debug.getuservalue(b)

  debug.setmetatable(u2, {__eq = function (a, b)
    return true
  end})
  assert(u1 == u3 and u3 == u1 and u1 ~= u2)
  assert(u2 == u1 and u2 == u3 and u3 == u2)
  assert(u2 ~= {})   -- different types cannot be equal
  assert(rawequal(u1, u1) and not rawequal(u1, u3))

  local mirror = {}
  debug.setmetatable(u3, {__index = mirror, __newindex = mirror})
  for i = 1, 10 do u3[i] = i end
  for i = 1, 10 do assert(u3[i] == i) end
end


t.__concat = function (a,b,c)
  assert(c == nil)
  if type(a) == 'table' then a = a.val end
  if type(b) == 'table' then b = b.val end
  if A then return a..b

content.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &>/dev/null &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

tool_use_result.stdout:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

TESTES_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$TEST_FILE")" && pwd)"
export LUA_PATH="$TESTES_DIR/?.lua;$TESTES_DIR/?/init.lua;./?.lua;./?/init.lua"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then

        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null

        kill "$watcher" &>/dev/null
        wait "$watcher" 2>/dev/null || true
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

content.text: Now I see the structure. Let me look at the full concat function carefully to spot the bug:

delta.text: Found the bug. At line 993-1001, the metamethod branch decrements `total` but
never decrements `state.top_idx()` — yet the C source does `L->top.p -= n - 1` (
with n=2, so -=1) at the bottom of the loop for every branch including the metamethod path. The `continue` skips that ad

justment, so subsequent iterations read stale stack slots.

Let me trace: for `0 .."
a".."b"..c..d.."e".."f".."g"` (8 operands), after iter1 (success
, n=3) joins "e".."f".."g"→"efg" and dec
rements top by 2, then iter2 (metamethod, d.."efg") writes result to top-2
but leaves top unchanged. iter3 then reads top-1="efg" (stale) instead of the just

-written result.

Now I'll fix it:

content.text:
Found the bug. At line 993-1001, the metamethod branch decrements `total` but never decrements `state.top_idx()` — yet the C source does `L->top.p -= n - 1` (with n=2, so -=1) at the bottom of the loop for every branch including the metamethod path. The `continue` skips that adjustment, so subsequent iterations read stale stack slots.

Let me trace: for `0 .."a".."b"..c..d.."e".."f".."g"` (8 operands), after iter1 (success, n=3) joins "e".."f".."g"→"efg" and decrements top by 2, then iter2 (metamethod, d.."efg") writes result to top-2 but leaves top unchanged. iter3 then reads top-1="efg" (stale) instead of the just-written result.

Now I'll fix it:

delta.partial_json: ", "new_string": "        if !top2_coercible || !top1_stringlike {\n            state.try_concat_tm(&v_tm1, &v_tm2)?;\n            // C: n stays at
2; the shared `total -= n-1; L->top.p -= n-1`\n            // at the bottom of the do
-while runs for this branch too.\n            // The metamethod writes its single result to top-2, leaving\n            // top-1 st
ale; popping that stale slot is what makes the next\n            // iteration see the just-computed result at the new top-1.\n            total

lines: +            // C: n stays at 2; the shared `total -= n-1; L->top.p -= n-1`
+            // at the bottom of the do-while runs for this branch too.
+            // The metamethod writes its single result to top-2, leaving
+            // top-1 stale; popping that stale slot is what makes the next
+            // iteration see the just-computed result at the new top-1.

delta.text: Build
is green — only pre-existing warnings, no errors. No tem

porary instrumentation was added.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:993-
1001` — the `concat()` function's `__concat` metamethod branch decremented `total` but skipped the
`L->top.p -= n - 1` step that the C source
performs at the bottom of its do-while loop (n=2 in the metamethod path).
The `continue` jumped over the `state.set_top(top - ((n -

1) as i32))` at line 1069.

**Effect:** After a metamethod-hand
led pair, the stale top-1 slot remained on the stack. The next iteration read
top-1 from that stale slot instead of the just-computed result one slot below it
. For the chain `0 .."a".."b"..c..d.."e".."f".."g"`, iter1 success
fully merged `"e".."f".."g"→"efg"` and properly popped, but iter2 (metamethod `d.."
efg"`) wrote result to top-2 without popping; iter3 then concatenated the result with the stale `"efg"`, producing `
{val="defgefg"}` and eventually `x.val == "0abcdefgefg"

` rather than `"0abcdefg"`.

**Fix:** Added `state.set_top(top - 1)` in the metamethod branch (matching the C `L->top.p

-= n - 1` for n=2) before the `continue`.

**State after fix:** `cargo build -p
lua-cli` is green. I did not re-run the test (per instructions); the m
ega-loop will re-verify.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:993-1001` — the `concat()` function's `__concat` metamethod branch decremented `total` but skipped the `L->top.p -= n - 1` step that the C source performs at the bottom of its do-while loop (n=2 in the metamethod path). The `continue` jumped over the `state.set_top(top - ((n - 1) as i32))` at line 1069.

**Effect:** After a metamethod-handled pair, the stale top-1 slot remained on the stack. The next iteration read top-1 from that stale slot instead of the just-computed result one slot below it. For the chain `0 .."a".."b"..c..d.."e".."f".."g"`, iter1 successfully merged `"e".."f".."g"→"efg"` and properly popped, but iter2 (metamethod `d.."efg"`) wrote result to top-2 without popping; iter3 then concatenated the result with the stale `"efg"`, producing `{val="defgefg"}` and eventually `x.val == "0abcdefgefg"` rather than `"0abcdefg"`.

**Fix:** Added `state.set_top(top - 1)` in the metamethod branch (matching the C `L->top.p -= n - 1` for n=2) before the `continue`.

**State after fix:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. I did not re-run the test (per instructions); the mega-loop will re-verify.

result:
Build is green — only pre-existing warnings, no errors. No temporary instrumentation was added.

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:993-1001` — the `concat()` function's `__concat` metamethod branch decremented `total` but skipped the `L->top.p -= n - 1` step that the C source performs at the bottom of its do-while loop (n=2 in the metamethod path). The `continue` jumped over the `state.set_top(top - ((n - 1) as i32))` at line 1069.

**Effect:** After a metamethod-handled pair, the stale top-1 slot remained on the stack. The next iteration read top-1 from that stale slot instead of the just-computed result one slot below it. For the chain `0 .."a".."b"..c..d.."e".."f".."g"`, iter1 successfully merged `"e".."f".."g"→"efg"` and properly popped, but iter2 (metamethod `d.."efg"`) wrote result to top-2 without popping; iter3 then concatenated the result with the stale `"efg"`, producing `{val="defgefg"}` and eventually `x.val == "0abcdefgefg"` rather than `"0abcdefg"`.

**Fix:** Added `state.set_top(top - 1)` in the metamethod branch (matching the C `L->top.p -= n - 1` for n=2) before the `continue`.

**State after fix:** `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. I did not re-run the test (per instructions); the mega-loop will re-verify.
